details: Confirmed workspace structure for HOLMES and SIGMA_rule_extract.
Used /home/work/SIGMA/SIGMA_rule_extract as the main working tree.
Updated scripts/qa/rule_linter.py so empty subject constraints are allowed when object constraints are specific.
Linter now only errors when both subject_attributes and object_attributes are empty or wildcard-only.
This removed the previous mismatch between builder/validator and linter behavior.
Updated scripts/qa/llm_batch_reviewer.py system prompt so FAIL_TOO_BROAD only triggers when both subject and object are empty or wildcard-only.
Set up and used Ollama with qwen2.5-coder:14b for local QA review runs.
Initial holmes_full QA result: PASS 2907, FAIL 185.
After builder retagging pass 1: PASS 3028, FAIL 64.
After builder retagging pass 2: PASS 3050, FAIL 42.
EMPTY_OBJECT remediation and manual fixes further improved isolated problem cases.
Expanded subject/object type inference in scripts/translator/builder.py.
Added heuristics for network, registry, named pipe, process, identity, resource, cloud resource, and event-style log sources.
Retagged existing holmes_full outputs using updated heuristics.
Re-ran selected Sigma sources to recover FAIL_EMPTY_OBJECT cases.
Manually corrected several stubborn rules where object extraction remained empty.
Reduced EMPTY_OBJECT issues and converted some cases into more specific mismatch classes.
Generated 4 random JSON bundles with 25 rules each from holmes_full.
Sampling seed fixed to 20260406 for reproducibility.
Overhauled HOLMES_SYSTEM_PROMPT in scripts/translator/prompts.py.
Added STRICT SCHEMA RULES section.
Added NEGATIVE CONSTRAINTS & FORMATTING section.
Normalized output example to use canonical CommandLine casing.
Added few-shot critical examples for ImageLoaded and ImagePath/ServiceFileName placement.
Added builder-side forbidden subject key relocation safety net.
Relocation target list: imageloaded, imagepath, servicefilename, targetobject, targetfilename.
Extended FILE_KEYS and related inference so relocated keys are typed correctly.
Validated prompt behavior on random 20-rule samples using qwen2.5-coder:14b.
Before relocation safety net, prompt-only validation still showed subject drift.
After few-shot plus builder relocation, forbidden subject drift for ImageLoaded/ImagePath/ServiceFileName/TargetObject/TargetFilename dropped to 0 in the sampled final HOLMES output.
Added canonical subject allow-list entries for PE metadata and Windows event fields.
Added keys: description, product, company, eventid, provider_name, logname, channel.
Updated run_rule_translation.py to count Too Broad drops separately from ordinary failures.
Started a fresh full translation run over all YAML rules into rules/holmes_full_run20260406.

notes: Too Broad entries are expected defensive drops, not ordinary translation failures.
translation_errors.log contains both Too Broad drops and other processing failures.

notes_for_next_session: Wait for the full translation run in rules/holmes_full_run20260406 to finish.
After completion, capture final generated JSON count, Too Broad drop count, and other failure count.
Then run the linter summary and, if needed, another 14B QA pass on the new full output.
